🚨🤥 **How Trump ended up begging Iran for a ceasefire – not the other way around**

For weeks, Donald Trump told the world Iran was desperate, saying "They are begging to make a deal. Not me." The Financial Times now reports the opposite.

🔴  Trump had been pushing for a ceasefire for weeks to alleviate the economic strain caused by Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz, and depending on Pakistan for mediation

🔴  Even when Trump first threatened to bomb Iran's power plants on March 21, according to five people familiar with the back-channel communication, he was already privately pushing for a ceasefire

🔴  Strain was real: Iran had choked off the Strait of Hormuz, oil prices were spiking, markets were tanking, and Trump needed an off-ramp

🔴  Enter Pakistan: Army Chief Asim Munir shuttled messages between Iranian officials, Steve Witkoff, JD Vance, and Trump himself—even after the president threatened to "wipe out Iranian civilization"

The ceasefire wasn't a victory Trump forced on a broken Iran, but rather a lifeline he asked for. Now talks are set for Saturday in Islamabad, Pakistan.

🔍 **How the Davos and Bilderberg cabal eat away at your freedom, wealth and future**

Global wealth is increasingly concentrated in the hands of a few who use it to further entrench their power and expand their influence.

**Where is the trend going, and what risks does it pose?**

🔶  Globally, billionaire wealth surged 16% in 2025 to a record $18.3 trillion—three times the average annual growth of the past five years, according to British charity Oxfam’s annual report

🔶  Some 68% of billionaires—holding 77% of total billionaire wealth—live in Western countries

🔶  Since 2020, billionaire wealth has jumped by 81%

💰 Most billionaire wealth is ‘unearned’:

➡️ gained by inheritance, cronyism and monopoly power

➡️  rooted in colonial-era practices like the slave trade and land grabs

💵 The super-rich increasingly grow wealth through stocks and ownership—not investment in the Global South

Farewell, democracy: the rich are becoming politically dominant
 
🔴 Billionaires are over 4,000 times more likely to hold political office than ordinary people, Oxfam reports

🔴  The super-rich __boost their political power by:__

➡️  funding political candidates and buying elections

➡️  investing in projects that legitimize elite power

➡️  owning the [largest media companies](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/56656) and AI systems

➡️  influencing education and academia

🔴 Western tycoons curb freedom of speech on social media platforms, like [Larry Ellison with TikTok](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/63498) and [Elon Musk with X](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/59880)

🔴 Search engines and AI algorithms are being manipulated to keep inconvenient truths out of sight

🔴 Projects such as [digital ID,](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/64682) civilian databases and [round-the-clock surveillance](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/60080) are being promoted to control populations

🔴 [Narratives of ‘overpopulation’](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/61015), climate change and pandemics are used to shape public behavior

🪖 **Wars as a way to preserve wealth**

🌏 The US has announced it will automatically register men aged 18 to 26 for the military draft starting in December 2026

🌏 It increasingly projects military power, while the European Union strengthens its defense sector and mulls developing its own nuclear weapons

🌏 What’s behind all that? A drive to maintain control over global resources, sustain neo-colonial influence and preserve ‘unearned’ wealth

🚨 **Israel admits: Lebanon civil war was 'the goal from the start'**

Israeli TV reporter Alon Ben David says pushing Lebanon toward civil war has been the plan all along. Analyst Raviv Drucker agrees: 

"Maybe it's not so bad for us. Let the Lebanese government fight Hezbollah."

Destabilize the country. Force its government to fight the militia born of Israeli occupation. Gaza, Syria, Iraq — same playbook. Lebanon was always next. Now they're admitting it on air.

full_text:
📹 **Iran's lead negotiator is the man behind the country's 'missile cities**'

Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, the current Speaker of Parliament, served as the commander of the IRGC Aerospace Force from 1997 to 2000.

Amir Ali Hajizadeh, the former commander of the IRGC Aerospace Force who was killed in 2025, called Ghalibaf "the mastermind of the missile cities."

✈️ Those underground launch networks, which are capable of striking anywhere in the region, began taking shape under his leadership.

His deputy was Ali Abdollahi, who is now head of Khatam al-Anbiya. Other key contributors included Hassan Tehrani Moghaddam, the designer of Iran's ballistic missile project, and Commander Hajizadeh.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 📹 **Iran's lead negotiator is the man behind the country's 'missile cities**'
